posts_in_12h_window.text: #Axissurge. #foildrive - Okay so heres the single wave taken out of an entire session. 71kg, Gen 2.5. Stat's for the Surge 890, Max speed 33.3km/hr on a 560m ride. Average 25km/hr. Perfect wing for these waves. Head+ wave. I waited for a bigger one. Full session before this 31km/hr Max. Smaller shoulder high waves. Short fuse and 300p. I still think the 300p is good for the 890 and 830. I think thats the Max though before I go to the 830. Smaller day and longer fatter sections so 890 suits that better.
posts_in_12h_window.product_intel: Surge 890 maxes out at 33.3km/hr on single wave, 560m ride. Prefers 890 over 830 for smaller/longer section waves at 71kg.

comments_list.text: Interesting, I'm a bit lighter 66kg and finding 830 surge feels too big on anything over shoulder high. Tried 300 surf skinny, 300P and Skinny 35 and 45 all on ultrashort advance +. Plan on trying Sillyshort A+ had great success with that using Fireball 880 1000 and 1160. Have been thinking about getting 780 or 740 for anything over waist high. Just don't feel I can relax on the 830, gets this kinda weird fluttery feeling at top speed that I never seem to have with the Fireballs. Wonder if the weight of the Foil Drive calms things down a bit...
Nice. We have real long lefts where I ride, 890 is consistently high 30s while the 830 is normally in the low 40s top speed wise. Still feel totally in control
comments_list.note: 830 reaches low 40s km/hr on long lefts, 890 is high 30s

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: Wanted BSC 970 Australia

Advise please: I have a ARTpro1401 for light wind winging and loving it. For those zero wind days i want to try pump foiling on it. I have a Axis Hybrid midlength board and a SUP paddle to paddle up and then pump. What fuse and stab would people recommend to start my pump only journey, please? The choices are: Fuse - short or UltraShort. Stabilizer - pump-460, Progressive 425, Progressive 350, Progressive 275, skinny 360/45, Surf skinny 300/45

Hi there, Looking to buy a Surge 830 and wondering if you have some thoughts on this foil vs the ART 939 V2. I have the 939 V2, and dont love the turning but the lift and glide and speed are perfect. For my weight (92KG) and conditions (20-35 Kn, shoulder hi winging) Would the Surge 830 have similar lower end? Does the Surge model have better or worse low end than the V2?
posts_outside_12h_window_captured.comments_summary: Brad Gordon: 830 is 20-30% better than Spitfire/ART v2. Matt Stockton: Surge better in every way for pumping and wave riding, transition rail-to-rail very smooth.

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: I use the Axis K/S Series 19mm Freeride Foil Mast in 82cm. After 1 year riding 2x/week, one of the 2 long screws to attach the fuselage cannot be tightened. When I turn the screw, I can 'endless' tighten it. Thread seems stripped. Weeks before, screws didn't go in easy. Used Tef Gel. Mast is only used in sweet water.

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: Surge comparison 830 to 890. #Foildrive. Rider 72kg. Set up is Axis Aluminum integrated mast max system. I have been riding the Surge 890 for some time, been my go to wing. But getting cooked at 30+ km/hr on more solid swells. 25km/hr sweet zone for small to medium waves. As wave size increases so does speed especially on drop. I noticed at 30-35km/hr I struggled and had to get right on the front foot. So today did 2 batteries on...
posts_outside_12h_window_captured.product_intel: 890 gets challenging at 30-35 km/hr on solid swell; switching to 830 tested. 25km/hr is sweet zone for 890.

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: Foildrive session with some team riders from Armstrong... with the inventor of the Airchair, Mike Murphy, as our boat driver.

Shout out to anyone still riding one of these things in 2026 - the original AXIS DW board! With new boards going out of fashion before their paint job is even dry, 3 years is an eternity. It's such a great board - during the ongoing struggles I don't think I've ever felt that it's the board that's been holding me back
posts_outside_12h_window_captured.product_intel: Original AXIS DW board still praised in 2026 - longevity/timelessness highlighted

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.status: feeling happy at Osmosis Kiteboarding El Gouna
posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: Had an epic parawing downwind session in El Gouna (Red Sea, Egypt) in about 25 knots. Running the AXIS Fireball 1350 with a Skinny 45 stab on a 120L Frankboard. Really impressed again by the speed range and the maneuverability of the 1350 here. The Red Sea swell is relatively slow, but the Fireball still lets you accelerate when the wind picks up.
posts_outside_12h_window_captured.product_intel: Fireball 1350 + Skinny 45 on 120L Frankboard for para-wing DW in 25kn - praised for speed range and maneuverability

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: I have a question about the differences between the Fireball 880 (604cm²) and the Surge 740 (590cm²). They're about the same area but I prefer the Fireball's longer wingspan and higher AR. The Surge is obviously better at maneuvering on waves, but I don't think I'll really need that superpower. The 880 should be good enough at turning especially paired with surf skinny. The Fireball's gliding ability is just too good to miss.
posts_outside_12h_window_captured.product_intel: User prefers Fireball 880 for its wingspan and glide vs Surge 740 - interesting comparison request

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: The perfect board for winging for me. At 88kg, the Mini Dart 80L is stable, quick to take off, and nimble. And I love the new impact vest from the 2026 AXIS/Vaikobi collab. Saved my ribs already on a taco impact earlier this year.
posts_outside_12h_window_captured.product_intel: Mini Dart 80L praised at 88kg - stable, quick takeoff, nimble. AXIS/Vaikobi collab vest mentioned.

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: I've been wondering if it's worth keeping my Regular Black Short Fuse. I practice dockstart, wing foil, and sup foil. About 64kg. 1300v2 with Skinny 55 and Ultrashort Advance+ for sup foil and dockstart. I like this setup. I was using the Short with SF960 and Progressive 375 for wingfoil on a 67L ML board. Still learning to gybe. But found the board feels way better with the Ultrashort Adv+.
Surge 950 & 1010 are magical behind the boat. The footage is of the 1010 but was able to connect back to 3rd with the 950 with ease. The 1010 has a hair better glide...
posts_outside_12h_window_captured.product_intel: Surge 950 and 1010 praised for wake/boat tow. 1010 has slightly better glide.

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: The Surge (here 950) is a real all-around weapon. But for speed I still prefer the Tempo with lower drag.

posts_outside_12h_window_captured.text: Fatty & FB1350. Hell of a combo! https://www.instagram.com/p/DVWgMeZjG80/

I'm just starting to get good enough for DW Wingfoil runs. Trying to figure out foil size for consistent foiling while flagging out. General conditions are 1-2.5' bay bumps with wavelength from bump to bump max about 15'. Fetch about 10 miles. I'm 75kg, currently using surge 950, 320 surf skinny, 820pc mast, and +20 ultrashort fuse. When flagged out I'm finding it very hard to link bumps.

I've been on the Surge 890 for winging and Foil Drive in waves and love it. I'm 75kgs. Now thinking about more upwind/downwind runs on Foil Drive, with hopes of getting into downwind missions. The 890 pops up quick and has heaps of range, but debating: drop to 830 and pair 950 for light wind/downwind, or keep 890 and go big with 1010. I'm thinking 890 and 950 might be too close. What do you reckon?
Finally getting some proper Wakefoil sessions now that the lake season is here. Been on a tow in & foildrive fix for winter. The Surge 1010 been great for chasing down...
The Fatty mast is pretty sick! Mandatory for the 1750 but also so good with 1350. https://www.instagram.com/p/DVPCQqIjFDb/
posts_outside_12h_window_captured.product_intel: Fatty mast called 'mandatory' for Fireball 1750, also great with 1350
